'''Jordan Jamal Turner''' (born 9 January 1989) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who most recently played as a {{rlp|ce|so|lf}} or {{rlp|wg}}er for [[Oldham RLFC]] in [[RFL Championship]] and the [[Jamaica national rugby league team|Jamaica national team]].

He has also represented the [[England national rugby league team|England Knights]] at international level. He previously played for the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]], [[Hull F.C.]], [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] and the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]]. He spent time with the [[Canberra Raiders]] in the [[National Rugby League]], earning a place in the [[2017 All Stars match|World All Stars]] team in 2017.

==Background==
Turner was born in [[Oldham]], Greater Manchester, England. He attended [[the Radclyffe School]].

<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/raiders/nrl-2017-canberra-raiders-recruit-jordan-turner-has-a-fascinating-background/news-story/9c5a3d8bf904956f8888ffab77ceb7bc|title=NRL 2017: Canberra Raiders recruit Jordan Turner background heritage|date=5 December 2016|website=Foxsports.com.au|accessdate=9 October 2017}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Salford City Reds===
Turner first joined Salford City Reds at the age of 14 from his amateur club Waterhead A.R.L.F.C. (in [[Waterhead, Greater Manchester]], Oldham). He captained the England Under-18s team and was voted Academy Player and Young Player of the Year in 2007, before establishing himself in the Salford City Reds' first team for 2 seasons.
[[File:Jordan Turner.JPG|thumb|right|Turner playing for Hull F.C. in 2010]]

===Hull FC===
Turner joined Hull F.C. from Salford City Reds at the start of the 2010 season. The sought after signing is hoping for some successful years to come with Hull F.C. and has enjoyed runs at {{rlp|ce}} and {{rlp|so}} since throwing on the black and white jersey. Twice in 2012 Turner played for the [[England Knights]] team.
In July 2012, Turner signed a 2-year contract with St. Helens starting from 2013 season.<ref>{{cite news| url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/18686620| title=Jordan Turner| date= 4 July 2012}}</ref>

===St. Helens===
Turner was voted St Helens ''Player of the Season'' for 2013.

After 9 consecutive wins in the 2014 season (1 in the [[Challenge Cup]]) the Saints lost 2 consecutive games against [[Wigan Warriors]] and [[Widnes Vikings]]. Jordan was determined to make the club bounce back from two moral killer defeats. As of 24 April 2014, Jordan has scored three tries this season (all in the [[Super League]]) against Wigan, [[Catalans Dragons]] and [[Castleford Tigers]].

St Helens reached the [[2014 Super League Grand Final]], and Turner was selected to play at {{rlp|lf}} in their 14–6 victory over the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/11156623/St-Helens-14-Wigan-Warriors-6-Moment-of-madness-from-Wales-international-Ben-Flower-costs-Wigan-dear.html|title= St Helens 14 Wigan Warriors 6: Moment of madness from Wales international Ben Flower costs Wigan dear|date= 11 October 2014 |work=Daily Telegraph|accessdate=15 October 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2014/oct/11/st-helens-wigan-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title= St Helens win Grand Final after Wigan's Ben Flower is sent off|date= 11 October 2014 |work=Guardian |accessdate=15 October 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.bbc.com/sport/0/rugby-league/29559845|title= St Helens 14–6 Wigan Warriors|date= 11 October 2014 |work=BBC Sport|accessdate=15 October 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last1=Cartwright|first1=Phil|title=St Helens v Wigan as it happened|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/live/rugby-league/29416637|accessdate=12 October 2014|work=[[BBC Sport]]|publisher=[[BBC]]|date=11 October 2014}}</ref>

===Canberra Raiders===
On 14 July 2016 he signed with NRL club the [[Canberra Raiders]] for two seasons.<ref>{{cite news|title=Raiders sign Jordan Turner|url=https://www.nrl.com/raiders-sign-jordan-turner/tabid/10874/newsid/99192/default.aspx|accessdate=15 July 2016|website=NRL.com|date=15 July 2016}}</ref>

===Huddersfield Giants===
On 26 May 2017, it was announced that Turner had signed for [[Super League]] side the [[Huddersfield Giants]] on a three-and-a-half-year contract.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.giantsrl.com/news/article/6176/giants-bring-back-english-talent|title=Huddersfield Giants|website=Giantsrl.com|accessdate=9 October 2017}}</ref> He made his début on Sunday 4 June against [[Warrington Wolves]], scoring a try in the side's 44–4 home win.{{citation needed|date=March 2020}}

===Castleford Tigers===
In November 2020, it was announced that Turner had signed for the [[Castleford Tigers]] on a one-year deal.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2020-11-26 |title=Jordan Turner: Castleford Tigers sign Huddersfield Giants centre |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55073720 |access-date=2022-07-25 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |last=Craven |first=Dave |date=2020-11-26 |title=Castleford Tigers raid Huddersfield Giants again for Jordan Turner |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-raid-huddersfield-giants-again-jordan-turner-3048668 |access-date=2020-12-15 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref>

In May 2021, Turner signed a new two-year deal with Castleford.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-05-24 |title=Jordan Turner: Utility back signs new two-year Castleford Tigers contract |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/13995/12316124/jordan-turner-utility-back-signs-new-two-year-castleford-tigers-contract |access-date=2022-07-25 |website=Sky Sports}}</ref> In the [[2021 Challenge Cup]] semi-final, Turner scored a [[hat-trick]] as Castleford defeated [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] 35–20.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57345934|title=Challenge Cup semi-final: Castleford Tigers beat Warrington 35-20 to set up St Helens final|work=BBC Sport }}</ref> On 17 July 2021, he played for Castleford in their [[2021 Challenge Cup Final]] loss against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57828390|title=Challenge Cup: St Helens beat Castleford 26-12 for first Wembley victory since 2008|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

On 15 October 2021, Turner made his début for the [[Jamaica national rugby league team|Jamaica national team]] against the [[England Knights]] at [[Wheldon Road]] for his [[testimonial match]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-10-15 |title=Youthful England Knights defeat Jamaica |url=https://rugby-league.com/article/36158/youthful-england-knights-defeat-jamaica |access-date=2022-07-25 |website=www.rugby-league.com}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=2021-10-15 |title=Jordan Turner on Jamaica, representing his roots & inspiring the next generation |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/jordan-turner-on-jamaica-representing-his-roots-inspiring-the-next-generation/ |access-date=2022-07-25 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>
Turner played 20 games for Castleford in the [[Super League XXVIII]] season and as the club finished 11th on the table narrowly avoiding relegation.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-ace-alex-mellor-delivers-brutally-honest-assessment-of-awful-season-weve-massively/|title=Castleford Tigers ace Alex Mellor delivers brutally honest assessment of 'awful' season; 'We've massively underperformed'|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>

===Oldham RLFC===
On 23 August 2023, it was announced that Turner had signed for his hometown club [[Oldham RLFC]] on a two-year deal in a player-coach role.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/jordan-turner-super-league-stalwart-to-join-hometown-club-oldham|website=Love Rugby League|title=Jordan Turner: Super League stalwart to join hometown club in player-coach role in 2024|accessdate=28 September 2023|date=23 August 2023}}</ref>

On 28 August 2025, it was announced that Jordan had retired from playing rugby league with immediate effect.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=28 Aug 2025|title= Former St Helens and Hull star announces shock retirement as brutal physical condition revealed|url= https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-st-helens-hull-fc-star-jordan-turner-shock-retirement-oldham-championship|author=Aaron Bower }}</ref>
